methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Astrolight, a seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Laurynas Mačiulis, has raised €2.8M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace, spanning 23 firms in the Space & Aerospace sector.

Standard triangulation methods do not yield a reliable valuation anchor in this instance. Peer-median analysis is inapplicable: while 23 same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comps yields fewer than 3 datapoints—insufficient for a robust median. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be applied because Astrolight's revenue is not on file; this is typical of early-stage private companies. The public sector median P/S of 45.3x across the 6-ticker universe offers directional context only—the absence of revenue data precludes multiplication.

Given these constraints, a quantitative valuation range cannot be responsibly triangulated from the available data. The €2.8M already raised represents a floor but does not imply a post-money valuation without capitalization table detail. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, path to profitability, and whether Astrolight's business model aligns with capital-intensive hardware plays (SpaceX, Blue Origin) or asset-light services (Planet Labs). Further diligence on unit economics and market traction is essential before establishing a defensible valuation.
